title: Investing Capital to Defend the Nation
body: Author: David Ulevitch
Context: Capital investment in American industrial capacity as national security imperative

title: Agenda
body: U.S. technological leadership decline
Shift from ESG to Global Competition and Innovation framework
Eight critical sectors for investment
Role of institutional investors in national security

title: America's Technology Leadership Crisis
body: The U.S. led in 60 of 64 critical technologies in the early 2000s
Now the U.S. leads in only seven critical technologies
This represents a dramatic erosion of technological dominance over two decades

title: China's Technology Surge
body: China now leads in 57 of 64 critical technologies
This marks a near-complete reversal from the early 2000s balance
China's rise poses direct competitive challenge to U.S. technological supremacy

title: The Technology Leadership Gap
body: Early 2000s: U.S. led 60 of 64 technologies, China minimal
Today: U.S. leads only 7, China leads 57 of the same 64 critical technologies
The swing represents transfer of strategic advantage in most critical domains

title: Reframing Investment Strategy
body: Shift from ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) framework
To GCI: Global Competition and Innovation framework
New lens prioritizes competitive position and technological leadership

title: Critical Sector: Semiconductors
body: Semiconductors identified as first critical sector
Foundation technology for computing, AI, and defense systems
Requires sustained capital investment for domestic capacity

title: Critical Sector: Energy
body: Energy identified as critical investment sector
Energy scaling necessary for manufacturing and AI infrastructure
National security depends on reliable domestic energy capacity

title: Critical Sector: Artificial Intelligence
body: AI identified as critical technology sector
Requires capital for deployment at scale, not just research
AI dominance directly impacts military and economic competition

title: Critical Sector: Biotechnology
body: Biotech identified as critical investment sector
Strategic importance for health security and life sciences leadership
Part of eight-sector framework for competitive advantage

title: Critical Sector: Space
body: Space identified as critical investment sector
Strategic domain for communications, reconnaissance, and national defense
Requires long-term capital commitment for infrastructure

title: The Eight Critical Sectors
body: GCI framework identifies eight critical sectors total
Includes semiconductors, energy, AI, biotech, and space
Three additional sectors complete the strategic investment map

title: Institutional Investors as Anchors
body: Large institutional investors must anchor capital in critical sectors
Includes pension funds, sovereign wealth vehicles, insurance companies, and endowments
Their scale necessary to finance manufacturing, AI deployment, and energy infrastructure

title: Pension Funds and Sovereign Wealth
body: Pension funds identified as key capital source
Sovereign wealth vehicles must participate in national capacity building
Long-term horizons align with infrastructure investment timelines

title: Insurance and Endowment Capital
body: Insurance companies hold capital suitable for long-duration projects
Endowments can anchor critical technology and manufacturing investments
Both sectors essential to diversified institutional funding base

title: Three Investment Priorities
body: Manufacturing: rebuilding domestic production capacity
AI deployment: scaling artificial intelligence infrastructure
Energy scaling: expanding reliable domestic energy supply

title: Fiduciary Duty, Not Charity
body: This investment strategy is financially rational fiduciary duty
Not charity or subsidy, but sound long-term risk management
Returns aligned with securing competitive position and reducing strategic vulnerability

title: National Security Through Capital
body: Capital investment in industrial capacity is national security imperative
Institutional investors hold the scale to restore U.S. technological leadership
GCI framework aligns financial returns with strategic competition requirements
